text:
You are about to watch a documentary adaptation of Why Managing Economies Is Hard — an investigation into one of the most important and least honestly discussed questions in public life.

This is not a story about economic failure. It is a story about the permanent limits of economic knowledge — and about why the confidence with which economies are managed so rarely matches the humility the evidence demands.

And it is a story about what governing wisely, in the presence of genuine uncertainty, actually looks like.

Thank you for watching.

Economies are not machines. They are not puzzles with solutions. They are emergent expressions of human behaviour — adaptive, uncertain, and permanently beyond full control.

The goal is not to eliminate uncertainty. It is to govern wisely in its presence.

Why Managing Economies Is Hard. 2026.
